title: Westpac extends ‘DIDO’ banking
full_text:
Banking major Westpac is expanding its trial of drive-in-drive-out banking to communities without branches, with six new sites in Queensland set to get the service.
Westpac said it would launch the community banking services across six towns: Clifton, Pittsworth, Malanda, Ravenshoe, Clermont and Laidley.
It forms part of a push by Westpac to offer more services in the bush and across the regions, as the bank targets growth for its business and retail bank.
Westpac has already trialled its community banking service in NSW over recent months.
Under the model Westpac staff in its regional banking hubs will drive into other communities not currently serviced by the bank. They then offer in-person banking services.
Westpac chief executive Anthony Miller will appear in the regional Queensland city of Toowoomba on Monday as he announces the bank’s expansion.
Mr Miller said a single approach to offering banking to the bush wasn’t working, noting Westpac had to find ways to offer services in more towns.
“From agriculture to resources, tourism and small business, regional Queensland is central to the country’s growth and resilience,” he said.
“A one-size-fits-all model doesn’t always work, particularly across such diverse regional communities. The Community Banking Service is about being present in these regions, understanding local conditions and supporting everyday decisions, whether that’s for households or businesses.” Some Westpac bankers offering the service use bank-branded vans, while others have been hosted in council offices.
They will not provide cash services to customers.
After Queensland, Westpac is looking at rolling out its model to more states on the eastern seaboard.
“While most banking is now done digitally, people still like face-to-face service,” Mr Miller said. “The challenge and the opportunity we have is finding the right balance between in person and digital, so customers get the service they need in a way that makes sense for them.” Westpac will also invest $10m to reinvigorate six branches and business centres across regional Queensland in Roma, Cairns, Rockhampton and Dalby. A number of regional bank branches have been opened by Westpac under Mr Miller, including sites in Moree in NSW, Leongatha in Victoria, Smithton in Tasmania, and a planned site in Yass in NSW.
